title: I built an AI trading coach that actually reads your trade history and tells you what's killing your edge — not generic advice
author: Local-Amphibian9197
selftext:
Most "AI trading tools" either try to predict the market or give you the same recycled advice you'd find in any trading psychology book.

I wanted something different: an AI that has direct access to **my actual trades** — every entry, exit, RR, strategy, confluence, time of day, day of week — and uses that data to find patterns I can't see myself.

So I built it into my trading journal.

**How it works:**

The AI coach doesn't just see your P&L. It sees everything:

* Which strategies you're using and how each one performs
* Your win rate per strategy vs. the breakeven win rate (the formula: 1 / (1 + avg RR))
* What confluences were present on your winning trades vs. your losing trades
* What time of day you're profitable and when your edge disappears
* What day of the week you overtrade or underperform
* Your risk-to-reward patterns, streak data, average duration

Then it gives you feedback based on **your data**. Not "be patient" or "follow your plan" — actual, specific insights like:

*"Your OB strategy has a 43% win rate with a 1.3 avg RR — your breakeven is 43.5%. This strategy is currently bleeding money. Consider tightening your entry criteria or dropping it entirely."*

*"You take 40% of your trades on Fridays but your Friday win rate is 31%. Your week would be significantly more profitable if you stopped trading after Thursday."*

*"When you have 4+ confluences, your win rate jumps to 67%. With fewer than 3, it drops to 34%. Your edge is directly tied to setup quality — the data is clear."*

That kind of feedback is impossible to get from a generic AI prompt. It needs your actual trade history to say anything useful.

**The journal itself tracks:**

* Full trade logging — entries, exits, RR, P&L, strategy, symbol
* Custom confluence toggles — you define your setup criteria, toggle them per trade, then the AI (and the analytics) can cross-reference what works
* Performance breakdowns by strategy, symbol, day, hour — all filterable
* Breakeven win rate analysis that color-codes strategies as profitable / marginal / unprofitable
* Performance calendar — daily/monthly heatmap
* Risk calculator with live prices

The AI coach sits on top of all of this. It's not a chatbot that knows about "trading in general" — it's a coach that knows about **your trading specifically**.

**Early access:**

I'm launching on Feb 15 with 50 spots on the waitlist.

  
→ Everyone: 40% off for life + first month free **PREMIUM**

The Basic plan (free forever) includes full journaling and analytics. The AI coach is on the Pro plan — and if you're on the waitlist, you get to try it free.

[www.tradingsfx.com](http://www.tradingsfx.com)

For anyone here who's into building AI tools for trading — I'd love to hear how you approach it. The model works way better when it has structured data to analyze (confluences, RR, strategy tags) vs. just raw entries and P&L. The structure of the input matters more than the model itself, at least in my experience.

What would you want an AI coach to tell you about your trading?

comments.author: bostrovsky
comments.body: I have been building (for a while now) a backend tool for the Tasty platform that downloads all your trades (initially everything to date and then incrementally from the last sync) and assembles the whole trade from inception through rolls, corp actions, closes, expirations, etc. and figures out the initial strategy (strangle, condor, etc.) It also tracks fees and puts together a graph along with historical data for the underlying and options (assuming it is an option trade).  This has been quite a task as brokerage data comes from multiple places in very different formats.  Pulling it all together so you can visually see what happened.  The next step is to do something like what you are describing. It also has an LLM attach so you can get insights from the trades but I haven't yet tried to get to summary recommendations or coaching.  Baby steps...
